seoTitle: How to Change Your Logo in Brevo
metaDescription: Update your brand logo in Brevo globally through the Brand Library or inside individual email templates, with proper scaling and responsive sizing.
intro: Refreshing your business branding or swapping outdated graphics shouldn't mean manually replacing images across hundreds of drafts. Brevo lets you update your logo either globally through the centralized Brand Library or individually inside a specific newsletter layout, keeping your visual identity consistent without extra rework.

steps.heading: Open the design canvas
steps.body: Launch the layout design canvas for the campaign or template where you want to update the logo.

steps.heading: Update the global Brand Library
steps.body: For a site-wide change, modify your primary corporate logo through the Style configuration block so it updates across future email mockups automatically.

steps.heading: Swap an individual layout file
steps.body: For a one-off change, click the existing header graphic inside a specific campaign and upload the replacement image directly.

steps.heading: Adjust scaling and padding
steps.body: Fine-tune pixel widths and padding so the new logo displays cleanly without stretching or distortion.

steps.heading: Test layout responsiveness
steps.body: Preview the updated logo across both desktop and mobile views to confirm it renders correctly before sending.

faq.question: Will changing my logo in the Brand Library update old campaigns too?
faq.answer: No, updating the Brand Library generally affects new templates and mockups going forward, not campaigns that were already sent.

faq.question: Can I use a different logo for a single email only?
faq.answer: Yes, you can swap the logo inside an individual layout without changing your global Brand Library settings.

faq.question: What image format should I use for my logo?
faq.answer: Crisp PNG or vector files typically produce the cleanest results without pixelation.

faq.question: Why does my logo look stretched after uploading?
faq.answer: This usually happens when the pixel width isn't adjusted to match the image's aspect ratio, so check the sizing settings after upload.
